mnemonics.title: Mnemonic 1
mnemonics.content: P(X=x, Y=y) = P(X=x AND Y=y): Use 'AND' to remember Joint Probability is the probability of both events occurring together.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 1
mnemonics.content: The joint PMF, p_X,Y(x,y), gives the probability that random variable X takes the value x and Y takes the value y simultaneously.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 2
mnemonics.content: Marginal distributions are found by summing the joint PMF over all possible values of the other variable (e.g., p_X(x) = Σ p_X,Y(x,y) for all y).

mnemonics.title: Key Point 3
mnemonics.content: Two discrete random variables are independent if and only if their joint PMF factors into the product of their marginal PMFs: p_X,Y(x,y) = p_X(x) * p_Y(y) for all x, y.
